define i32 @redundant_align_on_nested_gep_from_alloca(i64 %idx1, i64 %idx2) {
  %arr = alloca [10 x [20 x i32]], align 8
  %gep1 = getelementptr [10 x [20 x i32]], ptr %arr, i64 0, i64 %idx1

  call void @llvm.assume(i1 true) [ "align"(ptr %gep2, i32 4) ]
  %val = load i32, ptr %gep2, align 4
  ret i32 %val
}

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions GEPs but known tests only show single-level GEPs. This tests nested/multi-level GEP chains from alloca where alignment is preserved through multiple indexing operations.

declare void @llvm.assume(i1 noundef) #0

define i32 @redundant_align_on_nested_gep_from_alloca(i64 %idx1, i64 %idx2) {
  %arr = alloca [10 x [20 x i32]], align 8
  %gep1 = getelementptr [10 x [20 x i32]], ptr %arr, i64 0, i64 %idx1
  %gep2 = getelementptr [20 x i32], ptr %gep1, i64 0, i64 %idx2
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 true) [ "align"(ptr %gep2, i32 4) ]
  %val = load i32, ptr %gep2, align 4
  ret i32 %val
}

define i32 @redundant_align_on_nested_gep_from_alloca(i64 %idx1, i64 %idx2) {
#0:
  %arr = alloca i64 800, align 8
  %gep1 = gep ptr %arr, 800 x i64 0, 80 x i64 %idx1
  %gep2 = gep ptr %gep1, 80 x i64 0, 4 x i64 %idx2
  assume_align ptr %gep2, i32 4
  %val = load i32, ptr %gep2, align 4
  ret i32 %val
}

#0:
  ret i32 undef
}

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary specifically mentions 'alloca pointers' but this test uses a global variable. The patch's getUnderlyingObject() handles globals, making the align assumption redundant since the global has 32-byte alignment.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
declare void @foo(ptr)

@global_array = global 400 bytes, align 32

define void @global_variable_gep_pointer() {
#0:
  %ptr = gep ptr @global_array, 400 x i64 0, 4 x i64 0
  assume_align ptr %ptr, i32 16
  call void @foo(ptr %ptr)
  ret void
}
=>
declare void @foo(ptr)

@global_array = global 400 bytes, align 32

define void @global_variable_gep_pointer() {
#0:
  call void @foo(nonnull ptr @global_array)
  ret void
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

tests.source_program:
declare void @llvm.assume(i1 noundef)
declare void @foo(ptr)

define void @bitcast_chain_pointer() {
  %alloca = alloca i8, i32 100, align 64
  %ptr1 = bitcast ptr %alloca to ptr
  %ptr2 = bitcast ptr %ptr1 to ptr
  %ptr3 = bitcast ptr %ptr2 to ptr
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 true) [ "align"(ptr %ptr3, i32 32) ]
  call void @foo(ptr %ptr3)
  ret void
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
declare void @llvm.assume(i1 noundef)
declare void @foo(ptr)

define void @bitcast_chain_pointer() {
  %alloca = alloca i8, i32 100, align 64
  %ptr1 = bitcast ptr %alloca to ptr
  %ptr2 = bitcast ptr %ptr1 to ptr
  %ptr3 = bitcast ptr %ptr2 to ptr
  call void @foo(ptr %ptr3)
  ret void
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary doesn't mention bitcast chains. The patch traverses through multiple bitcasts via getUnderlyingObject() to find the underlying alloca with 64-byte alignment, making the 32-byte assume redundant.

alive2.src:
declare void @llvm.assume(i1 noundef)
declare void @foo(ptr)

define void @bitcast_chain_pointer() {
  %alloca = alloca i8, i32 100, align 64
  %ptr1 = bitcast ptr %alloca to ptr
  %ptr2 = bitcast ptr %ptr1 to ptr
  %ptr3 = bitcast ptr %ptr2 to ptr
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 true) [ "align"(ptr %ptr3, i32 32) ]
  call void @foo(ptr %ptr3)
  ret void
}
